title: Scope service DB credentials per ControlPlane and database
summary:
Security review of CC-0116 (per-ControlPlane service DB-credential scoping). My focus area (tenant isolation/blast radius, secrets handling, RBAC, OpenBao policies/least-privilege, TLS, injection) is CLEAN. Per-ControlPlane isolation is correctly implemented and proven: dbCredentialRemoteKeyFor embeds BOTH cp.Namespace and cp.Name ('openstack/keystone/%s/%s/db'), exactly mirroring the admin precedent adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor ('openstack/keystone/%s/%s/admin/app-credential'); dbCredentialSecretName derives from keystoneName(cp) (cp.Name + '-keystone-db-credentials'). The data-driven TestDBCredentialRemoteKeyFor_And_SecretName_DistinctPerControlPlane proves no two ControlPlanes collide on either the OpenBao path or the materialised Secret, so the old shared blast radius of the deleted flat 'openstack/keystone/db' ExternalSecret is eliminated. No secret values are logged or hardcoded: the two G101 nolint annotations (dbCredentialSecretNameSuffix='-db-credentials', conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady='DBCredentialsReady') are legitimate false positives. The bootstrap DB password is generated in-pod via the @generate marker (sys/tools/random), never passed as a kubectl-exec CLI arg, and the kv_path is injected via env var to avoid shell injection. Both changed OpenBao policies (eso-management.hcl, eso-control-plane.hcl) remain READ-ONLY with only comment additions and no path widening; eso-management correctly scopes to keystone/* (least privilege) rather than openstack/*. The ExternalSecret is owner-referenced (SetControllerReference in the CreateOrUpdate mutate closure), CreationPolicy Owner, ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store. The extra mark_eso_managed on the read-only DB path is inert and not a security problem: no push policy grants write to the 'db' leaf (push-keystone-keys.hcl globs require a literal /fernet-keys or /credential-keys suffix), so no ESO/PushSecret token can ever overwrite the DB credential. APPROVED for the security slice.

Quality/architecture/DRY/YAGNI slice of CC-0116. The new reconcile_dbcredentials.go is a clean, faithful mirror of the two reference shapes: its ExternalSecret construction matches ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret (reconcile_korc.go:1204) — esov1 API, ClusterSecretStore ref to openBaoClusterStoreName, RefreshInterval 1h, Target.CreationPolicy Owner, SetControllerReference inside the CreateOrUpdate mutate closure (line 106) — and its wait/condition flow matches reconcileAdminCredential (WaitForExternalSecret -> not-ready requeue -> True). The pure builder dbCredentialExternalSecret (lines 52-67) carries no owner reference (verified: no SetControllerReference/OwnerReferences in the builder) and is reused by the test helper readyDBCredES for shape assertions, exactly as the builder doc-comment claims. All five SetCondition call sites set the full 5-field contract including ObservedGeneration: cp.Generation. Naming/constants are disciplined: a dedicated dbCredentialsRequeueAfter (10s) constant with a justifying DECISION comment, and the condition type is the const conditionTypeDBCredentialsReady (controlplane_controller.go:48), never an inline literal, registered in subConditionTypes index 1 and subReconcilerConditionTypes['DBCredentials']. The Keystone projection override (reconcile_keystone.go:123-124) is managed-only (clusterRef != nil guard), mutates only the projected child's value-typed SecretRef (DatabaseSpec.SecretRef is a value field, so the line-113 struct copy isolates it), leaving cp.Spec untouched — corroborated by the dedicated test assertion at reconcile_keystone_test.go:421-422. Docs (controlplane-reconciler.md sub-reconciler table: gate=none, requeue 10s, name {name}-keystone-db-credentials; controlplane-crd.md brownfield must-supply-secretRef contract) are accurate against the code. DRY is good: no duplicated helper logic, remote-key/secret-name builders are single-source. Detected patterns (per-cr-openbao-remotekey-builder, sub-reconciler-condition-contract-with-observedgeneration) already exist and are NOT re-emitted. One LOW finding: an admittedly-inert mark_eso_managed call in write-bootstrap-secrets.sh with an unresolved 'Reviewer: please verify' DECISION comment, plus residual 'Reviewer: please verify' annotations in committed test/fixture code.

Test quality and coverage for CC-0116 (scope service DB credentials per ControlPlane) is strong and the slice is APPROVED. The unit suite (reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go) covers managed-creates-ES with specific store/target/data/owner-ref assertions, not-ready-requeues, ready-true, brownfield-no-ES, and a distinct-per-CP table test asserting both namespace-axis remote keys and name-axis secret names with explicit NotTo(Equal) pairs. Owner-ref is checked via metav1.GetControllerOf (name + Kind + *Controller==true), not a brittle index. reconcile_keystone_test.go ManagedOverridesDBSecretRef asserts the new per-CP secretRef Name+Key AND that cp.Spec.Infrastructure.Database.SecretRef.Name stays 'keystone-db' (the assert-absence-of-old-state / no-mutation guard), plus that the rest of the database spec still flows through so the test cannot pass by zeroing the struct; BrownfieldLeavesSuppliedSecretRef pins the user-supplied name+key untouched. The integration suite includes TestIntegration_DBCredentialsGate_BlocksKeystoneUntilSecretExists (Consistently 2s window proving NO premature Keystone CR, reason WaitingForDBCredentialSecret), TestIntegration_MultiControlPlane_DistinctDBCredentialPaths (distinct paths + distinct secret names), and FullReconcile + Minimal both asserting the projected Keystone secretRef Name==dbCredentialSecretName(cp) and Key=='password' while the Minimal test independently asserts cp-level db.SecretRef.Name==DefaultDatabaseSecretName is retained by the defaulting webhook. The chainsaw db-credential-scoping suite is a single consolidated script behind a belt-and-braces CRD+ClusterSecretStore presence guard; its only bounded loop (wait_ready_condition, 300s) is well under the 10m exec timeout. e2e fixtures (missing-secret, pod-security-restricted) are correctly re-pointed to the per-CP OpenBao path; brownfield-database is correctly left untouched (it uses no openstack/keystone path). Helm clusterrole_test.yaml and role_test.yaml add externalsecrets create/update contains-asserts and keep lengthEqual:15 unchanged. Only LOW/cosmetic nits found: a redundant Helm assertion duplicating an existing rule block, and two untested-but-sibling-consistent error branches plus an unasserted RefreshInterval.

tests_checklist.item: Per-ControlPlane isolation unit test exists and asserts distinct keys across namespaces and distinct secret names across names (TestDBCredentialRemoteKeyFor_And_SecretName_DistinctPerControlPlane)
Brownfield path test asserts NO ExternalSecret projected and DBCredentialsReady=True (no OpenBao reference)
Managed-mode test asserts owner-reference, CreationPolicy Owner, ClusterSecretStore ref, and per-CP remoteRef key for both username and password
e2e catch block dumps Secret with describe (keys only) and never echoes decoded secret values
e2e/missing-secret fixture updated from flat shared path to per-CP scoped path (no leftover shared-key consumer)
Sub-reconciler unit tests cover managed-create, not-ready-requeue, ready-true, and brownfield no-op paths (reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go)
Distinctness test asserts both equality of derived key/name AND inequality across CPs (TestDBCredentialRemoteKeyFor_And_SecretName_DistinctPerControlPlane)
Keystone override test asserts cp.Spec is NOT mutated (reconcile_keystone_test.go:421-422) and brownfield leaves user secretRef in place
Owner-ref controller flag asserted on the projected ExternalSecret (test lines 123-128)
Helm clusterrole/role tests assert externalsecrets create+update verbs present for CreateOrUpdate

reconcile_dbcredentials_test.go managed-creates-ES asserts store (ClusterSecretStore/openBaoClusterStoreName), target (name+CreatePolicyOwner), data (2 entries username/password with remoteKey+property), and owner-ref
Owner-ref asserted by name+Kind+controller flag via metav1.GetControllerOf, NOT a brittle slice index
not-ready-requeues test asserts RequeueAfter>0 AND condition False with reason WaitingForDBCredentialSecret
ready-true test asserts ctrl.Result{} (no requeue) AND condition True with reason DBCredentialsReady
brownfield-no-ES test asserts apierrors.IsNotFound on the ES AND DBCredentialsReady=True
distinct-per-CP table test asserts distinct remote keys across namespaces and distinct secret names across names with explicit NotTo(Equal)
Assertions are specific (exact values), not vacuous no-error-only
reconcile_keystone_test.go ManagedOverridesDBSecretRef asserts new secretRef present AND cp.Spec old 'keystone-db' unchanged (no-mutation / absence-of-old-state)
ManagedOverridesDBSecretRef also asserts ClusterRef still flows through (cannot pass by clearing struct)
BrownfieldLeavesSuppliedSecretRef pins user-supplied name+key untouched
integration_test.go DBCredentialsGate ordering test uses Consistently to prove no premature Keystone CR, reason WaitingForDBCredentialSecret
integration MultiControlPlane DistinctDBCredentialPaths asserts distinct paths and distinct secret names
integration FullReconcile + Minimal assert projected Keystone secretRef==dbCredentialSecretName and Key=='password'
integration Minimal asserts cp-level db.SecretRef.Name==DefaultDatabaseSecretName retained by defaulting webhook
DBCredentialsReady in subConditionTypes (index 1) covered by data-driven TestAggregateReady_* (iterate subConditionTypes)
DBCredentials in subReconcilerConditionTypes map; covered by TestSubReconcilerConditionTypesCoversAllNames (ground-truth, pass)
chainsaw db-credential-scoping: belt-and-braces presence guard (CRDs + ClusterSecretStore), single consolidated script
chainsaw worst-case bounded loop (wait_ready_condition 300s) < exec timeout (10m/600s)
e2e fixtures (missing-secret 01-late-secrets, pod-security-restricted) re-pointed to per-CP path openstack/keystone/openstack/controlplane/db
brownfield-database fixture untouched (no openstack/keystone path referenced; user-supplied)
Helm clusterrole_test.yaml + role_test.yaml externalsecrets create/update contains-assert added; lengthEqual rule count unchanged (15)
No brittle index assertions on owner references anywhere in the slice
No conditional-guard / tautological assertions in the new tests

code_quality_checklist.item: dbCredentialExternalSecret is a pure builder; owner-ref set in reconciler mutate closure (separation of concerns)
Condition messages/logs carry only metadata (names, paths, %v of K8s API errors), no secret payloads
reconcile_dbcredentials.go mirrors ensureKORCCloudsYAMLExternalSecret shape (esov1, ClusterSecretStore, RefreshInterval 1h, CreationPolicy Owner)
SetControllerReference is inside the CreateOrUpdate mutate closure; pure builder has no owner ref
Wait/condition flow mirrors reconcileAdminCredential (WaitForExternalSecret -> requeue -> True)
Condition type referenced via const, not inline literal (no-inline-literals drift guard respected)
Dedicated dbCredentialsRequeueAfter constant rather than reusing korcRequeueAfter, with justifying DECISION comment
Doc-comments are accurate and high quality (brownfield/managed control flow clearly documented)
No leftover unresolved DECISION ambiguity in production Go code
Residual 'Reviewer: please verify' annotation on an inert call in write-bootstrap-secrets.sh
Test helpers (dbCredManagedControlPlane, getDBCredES, readyDBCredES) are small, named, and mirror sibling reconciler test fixtures
Test comments map each test to REQ IDs and state intent (no copy-paste drift)

security_checklist.item: Tenant isolation: remote key embeds cp.Namespace AND cp.Name; no cross-CP collision (proven by distinctness test)
Secret name derived from keystoneName(cp) so two CPs in one namespace never share a materialised Secret
Matches admin-credential precedent (adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor) for scoping discipline
No hardcoded secrets; G101 nolints on suffix/condition-type are legitimate false positives
No secret values logged anywhere in reconcile_dbcredentials.go or WaitForExternalSecret
Bootstrap DB password generated in-pod via @generate (sys/tools/random), not a kubectl-exec CLI arg
kv_path injected via BAO_KV_PATH env var to prevent shell injection into sh -c
db_path seed mirrors admin_path seed (symmetric, idempotent write_secret_if_missing)
Extra mark_eso_managed on read-only DB path is inert: no push policy grants write to the db leaf; no exploitable capability
OpenBao eso-management.hcl read grants unchanged; trailing-* wildcard already covers per-CP path; no widening
eso-management scoped to keystone/* not openstack/* (least privilege; nova/neutron etc excluded)
eso-control-plane.hcl openstack/* read grant already covers per-CP db path; read-only; no widening
ExternalSecret owner-referenced via SetControllerReference (GC wired) and CreationPolicy Owner
ClusterSecretStore openbao-cluster-store used (TLS-backed cluster store, VAULT_CACERT in bootstrap)
RBAC not widened: helm only adds test assertions over the existing externalsecrets create/update rule reused from the admin AC ExternalSecret
cp.Spec untouched; per-CP secretRef override applied only to projected Keystone child under managed-mode guard (ClusterRef != nil)
Deleted keystone-db.yaml removed the shared flat-path ExternalSecret; kustomization entry removed (no dangling shared blast radius)
G101 false-positive suppressions are scoped and justified (suffix const + condition-type const)
No secret values asserted/logged in chainsaw (Secret keys-only describe; jsonpath checks key existence not value)

architecture_checklist.item: DBCredentials sub-reconciler ordered after Infrastructure and before Keystone so the scoped Secret materialises before Keystone consumes its secretRef
Sub-reconciler placed in dependency order (after Infrastructure, before Keystone) in the Reconcile chain
Condition registered in BOTH subConditionTypes (index 1) and subReconcilerConditionTypes['DBCredentials']
Managed/brownfield split follows the established clusterRef discriminator convention
Per-CR OpenBao remote key embeds {namespace}/{name} mirroring adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor
Projection override is value-copy isolated; cp.Spec is never mutated
Owner-ref/GC model documented and consistent (ESO owns materialised Secret via CreationPolicy Owner; ES owner-ref'd to CP)
Gate ordering (DBCredentials before Keystone) verified by a dedicated negative integration test, not only the happy path

dry_yagni_checklist.item: Reuses openBaoClusterStoreName, childNamespace, keystoneName, WaitForExternalSecret rather than reinventing
Mirrors admin-credential naming/scoping discipline instead of a divergent scheme
No duplicated ExternalSecret-building logic; builder/remote-key/secret-name helpers are single-source
Constants centralised in requeue_intervals.go; no magic-number requeue literals
No speculative/unused fields, flags, or helpers added (YAGNI respected)
Existing patterns NOT re-emitted (per-cr-openbao-remotekey-builder, sub-reconciler-condition-contract already present)
mark_eso_managed on the DB path is admitted-inert (no PushSecret targets it); arguably YAGNI but symmetry-justified
Multi-CP distinctness reuses driveControlPlaneToAdminCredentialReady rather than duplicating phase wiring
One redundant Helm assertion duplicates an existing rule block (see issues_found)

fail_fast_checklist.item: KORC_CONTROLPLANES identity validated to be '<namespace>/<controlplane>' form, exits 1 otherwise
ExternalSecret error and not-ready paths set DBCredentialsReady=False with distinct reasons and requeue/return error
Errors from CreateOrUpdate and WaitForExternalSecret set a False condition and return the error for controller-runtime backoff
Not-ready path requeues rather than silently proceeding, gating Keystone until the DB Secret exists
chainsaw script uses set -euo pipefail and assert_eq helper that exits 1 on mismatch
Presence guard exits 0 (SKIP) cleanly when stack absent, so failFast suite is not hard-failed by the new file

defensive_checklist.item: Non-generated bootstrap values single-quote-escaped before sh -c interpolation
WaitForExternalSecret inspects only conditions, never .Data; missing ES returns (false,nil) not a panic
Value-typed SecretRef copy prevents aliasing the source cp.Spec on override
Brownfield early-exit avoids referencing OpenBao/ClusterSecretStore when the user owns the credential
ExternalSecretError CreateOrUpdate-failure branch and WaitForExternalSecret-error branch are not unit-tested (sibling-consistent gap, see issues_found)

issues_found.description: The DB-path mark_eso_managed call in the bootstrap seed is documented as currently inert (no PushSecret targets the DB path; the push-keystone-db.hcl policy is deferred to issue #439) and carries an unresolved 'Reviewer: please verify this is intended.' annotation. The operator's DB-credential ExternalSecret PULLS from OpenBao with CreationPolicy Owner and never pushes, so the managed-by=external-secrets metadata marker (which only matters for ESO's PushSecret managed-by adoption guard) has no live consumer. The call is harmless and symmetry-justified, but it is dead/inert work today and the DECISION leaves an explicit open verify request rather than a resolved decision. Recommend either removing the call until a DB PushSecret exists (YAGNI), or converting the comment into a resolved decision (drop 'Reviewer: please verify', state it is intentionally pre-staged for issue #439).
issues_found.fix: Resolve the DECISION: either delete the mark_eso_managed "${db_path}" line (and its comment) until a PushSecret writes the DB path, or reword the comment to a closed decision (remove the 'Reviewer: please verify this is intended.' line and state it is deliberately pre-staged for issue #439's push-keystone-db.hcl).

issues_found.description: Several committed test and e2e-fixture files retain development-time 'Reviewer: please verify' annotations attached to DECISION comments whose decisions are themselves fully stated and resolved (no open or/vs ambiguity). Examples: integration_test.go:335 and :1086 (harness sync-simulation placement), and the e2e chainsaw fixtures (CR-equivalence and belt-and-braces presence guard). These are cosmetic residue from the authoring pass; the decisions do not leave genuine ambiguity, but the lingering 'please verify' prompts read as unfinished once merged.
issues_found.fix: Strip the trailing 'Reviewer: please verify.' sentences from the committed DECISION comments now that the decisions are settled, leaving the rationale intact.

issues_found.description: The new Helm test 'should allow create and update on externalsecrets so reconcileDBCredentials can CreateOrUpdate' asserts the EXACT same content block (identical apiGroups external-secrets.io, resources externalsecrets/pushsecrets, verbs get/list/watch/create/update/patch/delete) as the pre-existing test 'should grant full CRUD on externalsecrets and pushsecrets'. It is a redundant contains-assert on the same rule: it cannot fail unless the existing test also fails, so it adds no new coverage. It does not affect the lengthEqual:15 rule-count guard (it is a contains on existing rules, not a new rule). Cosmetic; consider asserting the create/update verbs more narrowly or dropping the duplicate.
issues_found.fix: Either remove the duplicate test, or change its intent so it distinctly fails if create/update are dropped while the other verbs remain (e.g. a separate minimal contains with only the create+update verbs would still be subsumed by the full-CRUD assert; the cleanest option is to delete the duplicate since the full-CRUD test already proves create+update are present).

issues_found.description: The reconcileDBCredentials error branches are not unit-tested: the CreateOrUpdate-failure path that sets DBCredentialsReady=False with reason ExternalSecretError, and the WaitForExternalSecret error path with the same reason. The happy paths (create, not-ready, ready, brownfield) are all covered. This is consistent with the sibling reconcilers (reconcileAdminCredential, reconcileKeystone error branches are likewise not error-injection-tested) and the fake client makes injecting these errors awkward, so it is a low-priority gap rather than a regression risk.
issues_found.fix: Optional: add a fake client with an interceptor (WithInterceptorFuncs) that returns an error on Create/Update or Get to assert the ExternalSecretError reason and that the call returns the error. Not blocking.

issues_found.description: The DB-credential ExternalSecret builder sets RefreshInterval to 1h (dbCredentialExternalSecret), but no unit test asserts the RefreshInterval value. The clouds.yaml ES sibling is similarly partial, so this is a minor completeness nit, not a coverage gap on load-bearing behavior (store/target/data/owner-ref ARE asserted).
issues_found.fix: Optional: add g.Expect(es.Spec.RefreshInterval).To(Equal(&metav1.Duration{Duration: time.Hour})) to the managed-creates-ES test.

suggested_improvements: Optional: the inert mark_eso_managed on db_path (write-bootstrap-secrets.sh:184) could be dropped until issue #439 wires a DB-rotation PushSecret, removing a no-op step and the 'Reviewer: please verify' marker. Not a security defect — it confers no write capability because no push policy grants write to the db leaf — but removing dead-until-#439 calls keeps the seed minimal. The accompanying DECISION comment already documents the rationale, so keeping it for symmetry is also acceptable.

Out of strict diff scope but worth a future tidy: push-keystone-keys.hcl comment (lines 33-48) still describes the read-only DB credential path as the older single-segment 'kv-v2/data/openstack/keystone/{namespace}/db'; CC-0116's actual DB path is namespace+name-scoped ('.../{namespace}/{name}/db'). The write globs require a literal /fernet-keys or /credential-keys suffix so neither shape can match — no functional or security impact — but the stale prose could mislead a future reader auditing blast radius.
Once issue #439 lands push-keystone-db.hcl and a DB-credential PushSecret, revisit the mark_eso_managed DB-path call so its presence is backed by a live consumer rather than pre-staged symmetry.

Consider a one-line note in reconcile_dbcredentials.go's builder comment that DatabaseSpec.SecretRef being a value (non-pointer) field is what makes the line-113 struct copy + line-124 reassignment non-aliasing — it is the load-bearing reason cp.Spec stays untouched and is currently only documented at the override site.

Consider deleting the duplicate externalsecrets create/update Helm assert in both clusterrole_test.yaml and role_test.yaml, since the existing full-CRUD assert already proves create+update are granted.
Optionally add an error-injection unit test (fake client interceptor) for the ExternalSecretError branch of reconcileDBCredentials to close the only untested code path in the new reconciler.
Optionally assert es.Spec.RefreshInterval==1h in the managed-creates-ES test for full builder shape coverage.
next_steps: No security blockers. Proceed; the per-CP isolation, least-privilege OpenBao policies, owner-ref/CreationPolicy Owner, in-pod password generation, and injection-safe bootstrap are all verified.
Optional: resolve/trim the residual 'Reviewer: please verify' DECISION annotations in committed code before merge; none block approval.
No blocking test-quality changes required for this slice; the three nits above are optional polish.

When the c5c3-operator + OpenBao stack is wired into kind E2E (documented CC-0110 fast-follow REQ-022/REQ-023), confirm the db-credential-scoping chainsaw test transitions from SKIP to exercising the assertions.

detected_patterns.name: per-tenant secret-path scoping by Namespace+Name (mirrors adminAppCredentialRemoteKeyFor)
ExternalSecret CreationPolicy Owner + controller owner-reference for GC
in-pod credential generation via @generate marker (no cleartext in process args)
env-var injection of untrusted path into sh -c (shell-injection avoidance)
read-only OpenBao policy with trailing-* wildcard pre-covering deeper per-CP paths (no widening)
least-privilege scoping keystone/* over openstack/* in eso-management
G101 nolint on name-suffix/condition-type string constants (documented false positives)
Data-driven aggregate tests iterate subConditionTypes so a newly added condition (DBCredentialsReady) is automatically covered without editing the test body.
Owner-reference assertions use metav1.GetControllerOf and check Kind+Name+*Controller==true rather than indexing OwnerReferences[0], avoiding brittleness against ordering.
No-mutation guard pattern: ManagedOverridesDBSecretRef asserts both the projected child's new value AND that the source cp.Spec value is unchanged, catching accidental in-place mutation.
Negative ordering test pattern: DBCredentialsGate uses Gomega Consistently over a bounded window to prove the gated child is NOT created prematurely, complementing the happy-path Eventually.
Multi-axis distinctness assertions: per-CP tests assert both the remote-key path (namespace+name scoped) and the materialised Secret name are distinct, with explicit NotTo(Equal) pairs.

Belt-and-braces chainsaw guard: a runtime CRD + ClusterSecretStore presence probe SKIPs cleanly (exit 0) under failFast when the operator stack is absent, with a single consolidated script step because chainsaw lacks step-level skip.

Bounded-loop timeout discipline: the only polling loop (300s) is kept well under the script/exec timeout (10m) so a stalled dependency surfaces as a clean assertion failure rather than a harness timeout.
